front: What is a structure in C programming?
back: A structure is a user-defined data type that allows grouping variables of different data types under a single name. It's used to represent a record.

front: What is a union in C programming?
back: A union is a special data type that allows storing different data types in the same memory location. Only one member can contain a value at any given time.

front: What is the key difference between a structure and a union?
back: In a structure, each member has its own memory location, while in a union, all members share the same memory location. The size of a structure is the sum of its members, while a union's size is that of its largest member.

front: How do you access members of a structure or union?
back: Members are accessed using the dot operator (.) for variables. If you have a pointer to the structure/union, you use the arrow operator (->).

front: Why would you use a union instead of a structure?
back: Unions are used for memory efficiency when you know that only one of several members will be used at a time. They are common in scenarios like representing different types of data in the same variable or for low-level system programming.
